channel.title: DysonX Public Signals
channel.description: Source-attributed public Signals tracking AI and AGI intelligence.

item.title: Agentic AI adoption accelerates from Codex usage evidence
item.description: A June 2026 research paper uses Codex usage data as evidence that agentic AI is moving from developer tooling toward broader organizational workflows.

item.title: AI Agent Index highlights transparency gaps in deployed agents
item.description: The 2025 AI Agent Index documents technical and safety features across deployed agentic AI systems and flags uneven transparency among developers.

item.title: EU AI Office becomes the GPAI governance surface to watch
item.description: The European AI Office supports AI Act implementation, especially general-purpose AI rules, evaluations, guidance, and enforcement workflows.

item.title: METR time-horizon work reframes autonomous capability tracking
item.description: METR tracks frontier AI progress through task-completion time horizons, measuring the length of tasks AI agents can complete at defined success rates.

item.title: NIST AI governance is anchored in standards
item.description: NIST frames AI governance around measurement, risk management, standards, guidelines, benchmarks, and evaluations.

item.title: How agents are transforming work
item.description: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.

item.title: Agent4cs: A Multi-agent System for Code Summarization in Large Hierarchical Codebases
item.description: arXiv:2607.01425v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Understanding large, complex codebases, especially those with obfuscated structures and incomplete documentation, remains a significant challenge. Existing code summarization solutions ofte...

item.title: Beyond Next-Token Prediction: An RLVR Proof of Concept for Tool-Use Agents on Atlassian Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2607.01465v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are trained to predict the next token, not to act inside a specific API. In niche enterprise SaaS workflows -- where success means hitting the right endpoint with the...

item.title: Janus: a Playground for User-Involved Agentic Permission Management
item.description: arXiv:2607.01510v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents that autonomously execute tool calls on a user's behalf raise pressing questions about permission management: what role could users play, and what role should they play? Despite m...

item.title: VLA-Corrector: Lightweight Detect-and-Correct Inference for Adaptive Action Horizon
item.description: arXiv:2607.01804v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) foundation models have recently achieved strong progress in embodied intelligence. To reduce policy-call frequency while preserving temporal coherence, most gen...

item.title: When Should Service Agents Reconsider? Difficulty-Routed Control in Customer-Service Operations
item.description: arXiv:2607.01426v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous customer-service agents are shifting from conversational interfaces toward operational execution roles: they retrieve firm records, apply service policies, and execute backend wr...

item.title: A Contextual-Bandit Oversight Game with Two-Sided Informational Asymmetry
item.description: arXiv:2607.00155v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study runtime human oversight of an AI agent when private information runs in both directions: the human privately knows her reward function, while the AI privately knows the quality of...

item.title: AGI Maze as a Benchmark Framework for World-Modeling Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.00627v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are powerful pattern-completion systems, but their default operating mode - predicting the next token from a static context - does not reliably produce persiste...

item.title: Dual-Informed Vertical Expansion for Multi-Objective Node Selection in Anytime Conflict-Based Search
item.description: arXiv:2607.00156v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Conflict-Based Search (CBS) is a leading exact algorithm for Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF), but its high-level node-selection rule is usually treated as a fixed implementation detail. Sta...

item.title: EgoSafetyBench: A Diagnostic Egocentric Video Benchmark for Evaluating Embodied VLMs as Runtime Safety Guards
item.description: arXiv:2607.00218v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) are now proposed as runtime safety guards for embodied agents in homes and factories. A deployable guard must catch genuinely unsafe situations while avoiding...

item.title: From Signals to Structure: How Memory Architecture Drives Language Emergence in LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.00233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How do two agents invent a shared language from scratch? In a Lewis signaling game, a sender and receiver must coordinate on a code using only their interaction history. We study five memor...

item.title: Managed Autonomy at Runtime: Gear-Based Safety and Governance for Single- and Multi-Agent Cyber-Physical Systems
item.description: arXiv:2607.00334v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous agents, whether LLM-driven software agents or robotic physical agents, face a common class of failure modes when operating without continuous human oversight: safety violations f...

item.title: Mnemosyne: Agentic Transaction Processing for Validating and Repairing AI-generated Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2607.00269v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs, solvers, and agent teams increasingly generate workflow actions, repairs, and plans, but a generated action may be syntactically valid yet stale, infeasible, conflicting, or destructi...

item.title: Personalization as Inverse Planning: Learning Latent Design Intents for Agentic Slide Generation via Structural Denoising
item.description: arXiv:2607.00407v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Slide design requires personalizing both deck themes and page layouts. Yet, current AI agent-based methods struggle with fine-grained, page-level design. Solely relying on prespecified temp...

item.title: PHREEQC-MCQ-200: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Tool-Augmented Scientific Simulator Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.00436v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model agents are increasingly connected to scientific software, yet it remains unclear when tool access makes scientific computation more reliable rather than merely more com...

item.title: Urban Deceleration Behavior Modes Under Scene Context: An Early-Kinematic Classifier from Argoverse 2 Multi-Agent Trajectories
item.description: arXiv:2607.00027v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Urban deceleration is one of the most empirically studied yet least taxonomically organized behaviors in car-following research. Recent perception-equipped autonomous-vehicle datasets enabl...

item.title: Mastering Agentic Techniques: AI Agent Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) is central to aligning language models, from reinforcement learning with human feedback (RLHF) within AI assistants to newer...

item.title: AgentBound: Verifiable Behavioral Governance for Autonomous AI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.30970v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous AI agents increasingly perform consequential actions on behalf of human principals, including financial transactions, external communications, and enterprise workflows. Existing...

item.title: AgRefactor: Self-Evolving Agentic Workflow for HLS Compatibility and Performance
item.description: arXiv:2606.30949v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: High-Level Synthesis (HLS) provides a fast path from concepts to silicon, but converting real-world software into synthesizable HLS code remains challenging due to restrictive language supp...

item.title: Auditing Generalization in AI-Generated Video Detection: A Six-Control Protocol and the VidAudit Toolkit
item.description: arXiv:2606.31004v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI-generated video detection benchmarks such as GenVidBench and AIGVDBench are the de facto leaderboards, yet most evaluation protocols leave uncontrolled confounds that can inflate reporte...

item.title: Contrastive Reflection for Iterative Prompt Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2606.30840v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM agents are becoming central to information retrieval: they issue retrieval queries, synthesize answers, and increasingly serve as judges for IR evaluation. Improving the prompts that co...

item.title: DSIP: A Dynamic Coordination Planner for Signal-Free Intersections using Diffusion-Model-Based Multi-Agent Motion Planning
item.description: arXiv:2606.30694v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traffic signal control at urban intersections inherently introduces stop-and-go behavior, resulting in increased delays and reduced traffic efficiency, especially under high traffic demand....

item.title: HyPOLE: Hyperproperty-Guided Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning under Partial Observation
item.description: arXiv:2606.30966v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Formal specification is a powerful tool to guide the learning process and provides significant advantages over reward shaping: (1) mathematical rigor; (2) expressiveness to specify objectiv...

item.title: OpenLife: Toward Open-World Artificial Life with Autonomous LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.31046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial life has explored life-like behavior on many computational substrates, but mostly in researcher-designed closed worlds. We argue that large language model (LLM) agents, with pers...

item.title: RoPoLL: Robust Panel of LLM Judges
item.description: arXiv:2606.30931v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The LLM Jury, a Panel of LLM Evaluators (PoLL) reporting consensus scores, has become a practical alternative to single-judge LLM evaluation, yet its statistical behavior remains poorly und...
